verdict_reasoning: HMGB1 is canonically a nuclear DNA-binding protein, but it is a prototypical DAMP/alarmin: it is actively secreted by activated immune cells (monocytes/macrophages) and passively released from necrotic/pyroptotic/necroptotic cells, and a translocated pool is displayed on the outer leaflet of stressed and dying cells (surface HMGB1 detected by flow on intact cells, and engaged by surface receptors RAGE/TLR4). This non-baseline ecto-display under cell-stress/death and immune-activation states qualifies as cell_state_induced surface accessibility. Anti-HMGB1 antibody programs exist but largely target the released soluble pool; the surface-displayed pool on stressed cells is documented.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Surface-displayed HMGB1 on stressed/dying cells is documented but the anchoring mechanism (lipid binding vs partner tethering) and wash-resistance are not fully defined.
